message:
fix(fcp-streaming): keep reconnect backoff on immediate Close (br-hjaej)

crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs ReconnectingWsStream tried to
reset reconnect backoff only after the first healthy message, but
poll_next called `note_message_received()` unconditionally on every
`Ok(Some(message))`, and WsConnection::recv marks the socket closed
before returning `Some(WsMessage::Close(...))` — so a peer that
completed the WS handshake and immediately sent Close still reset the
retry budget to zero. That defeated the 6e6cr reconnect hardening and
allowed near-hot reconnect loops against a peer that accepts then
instantly closes.

## Fix

`note_message_received` now takes `&WsMessage` and skips the reset
when `message.is_close()`. The reset gate
(`reset_backoff_after_first_message`) stays ARMED after a Close so a
subsequent data frame on the same session can still clear it.

## Regression tests (3 new + 1 migrated)

  reconnect_stream_close_frame_does_not_reset_backoff
    — the bead's canonical scenario; drives 3 failures, connects,
      receives Close, asserts attempts() still == 3 and the reset
      gate stays armed.

  reconnect_stream_close_frame_with_reason_also_preserves_backoff
    — same path for Close(Some(WsCloseFrame)) so a reason payload
      does not sneak through.

  reconnect_stream_data_message_after_close_still_resets_backoff
    — arm-once semantics: Close leaves the gate armed, and the
      first subsequent Text frame clears the retry budget as
      before.

  reconnect_stream_only_resets_backoff_after_first_message
    — existing test; updated to pass `&WsMessage::text("healthy")`
      to the new note_message_received signature.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --lib reconnect_stream
  -> 4 passed (1 pre-existing + 3 new).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --lib
  -> 522 passed (was 519 + 3 new); 1 pre-existing flaky timing
     failure (ws_connection_recv_times_out_when_peer_blackholes_
     after_handshake) reproduces on HEAD without these changes.

Closes flywheel_connectors-hjaej.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep clear of admin_state.rs overlap unless coordination changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:43:08.500400Z","created_by":"YellowSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:08.914806Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:08.914561Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","bughunt","fcp-host","rollout"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hhjnp","title":"fcp-audit: verify_chain accepts arbitrarily-future timestamps (no upper-bound sanity)","description":"## Finding
verify_chain (crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1081-1095) detects backward timestamp regressions: `if entry.occurred_at < prev.occurred_at { issues.push(... "audit.timestamp_regression" ...) }`. It accepts any forward-dated timestamp, including absurd futures (year 2096+ or u64::MAX).

## Consequences
1. **Freshness SLA poisoning.** FreshnessLevel + AuditStatus (lib.rs:1226-1302) compute freshness from the head timestamp. A single forged future-dated entry renders the chain "fresh" forever.
2. **Audit-query time-range poisoning.** Time-range filters return unexpected results.
3. **Monotonicity is preserved but meaningless.** An attacker who can inject one future entry can append arbitrarily many more with same-or-later timestamps and pass the regression check.

## Why this is a missing invariant
The current check establishes a lower bound (non-decreasing) but no upper bound. Calendar times outside a plausible window should be rejected. The verifier has no wall-clock input today.

## Recommendation
Add an optional `now_ceiling: Option<u64>` parameter (or a VerifyOptions struct) to verify_chain. When Some(now), reject entries with `occurred_at > now + clock_skew_grace` (e.g., 300 seconds). Emit "audit.timestamp_future" as a critical VerifyIssue. Leave None for offline audit replays with no wall clock.

Alternative minimum: add a static hard cap (`FUTURE_TIMESTAMP_HARD_CAP = 4_000_000_000` = year 2096) and reject anything beyond that statically. Crude but catches absurd cases.

## Evidence
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1081-1095 (regression check only)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1226-1302 (FreshnessLevel / AuditStatus consumers)
- No future-dated-entry rejection test exists (grep of lib.rs + tests/ for "timestamp_future" / "future_timestamp" returns zero hits).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:47:44.584375Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:33:58.012923Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:33:58.011273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2808,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hhjnp","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in 480020af. Added MAX_FUTURE_TIMESTAMP_SKEW_SECS=300 (Kerberos convention) and verify_chain_with_clock(entries, head, zone_id, now). Flags audit.timestamp_future as critical when occurred_at > now+skew. Uses saturating_add so u64::MAX is handled. Offline replays keep verify_chain. 368 lib tests pass; 4 new tests cover far-future, within-skew boundary, u64::MAX, and clean-chain agreement.","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:33:57Z"}]}

WsConnection::recv() at 651-666 marks the socket closed before returning Some(Close(...)), so a peer that completes the handshake and immediately sends Close still resets the retry budget to zero. That defeats the reconnect hardening from 6e6cr and allows near-hot reconnect loops against a peer that accepts then instantly closes. Real behavior should exclude close frames from backoff reset and cover handshake-success-then-immediate-close in tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:48.038143Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:33:49.281760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-hjaej","title":"[REVIEW-P5][MEDIUM] fcp-streaming::ReconnectingWsStream resets backoff on immediate close","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs:761-772 tries to reset reconnect backoff only after the first healthy message, but poll_next() at 823-826 calls note_message_received() for every Ok(Some(message)) without excluding control frames. WsConnection::recv() at 651-666 marks the socket closed before returning Some(Close(...)), so a peer that completes the handshake and immediately sends Close still resets the retry budget to zero. That defeats the reconnect hardening from 6e6cr and allows near-hot reconnect loops against a peer that accepts then instantly closes. Real behavior should exclude close frames from backoff reset and cover handshake-success-then-immediate-close in tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:48.038143Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:40:06.984790Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:40:06.984525Z","close_reason":"Changed ReconnectingWsStream::note_message_received to take the message by reference and skip the backoff reset when the message is a Close frame. Before the fix, a peer that completed the WS handshake and immediately sent Close would return Ok(Some(WsMessage::Close(...))) from recv(), which hit poll_next's Ok(Some(message)) arm and unconditionally reset the retry budget to zero — turning 'handshake-then-close' into a near-hot reconnect loop against the peer and defeating the 6e6cr hardening. Now close frames leave the retry budget intact, the reset gate stays armed, and the next real data frame on any subsequent successful connect can still clear it. 3 new regression tests: reconnect_stream_close_frame_does_not_reset_backoff (pins the bead scenario), reconnect_stream_close_frame_with_reason_also_preserves_backoff (Close with reason payload), reconnect_stream_data_message_after_close_still_resets_backoff (arm-once semantics preserved). Also updated the existing reconnect_stream_only_resets_backoff_after_first_message test to pass the new &WsMessage argument. Verified: 522 fcp-streaming lib tests pass (was 519 + 3 new), 1 pre-existing flaky timing failure (ws_connection_recv_times_out_when_peer_blackholes_after_handshake) is unrelated and reproduces on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hk0uj","title":"[SEC-AUDIT] fcp-host missing zone policy falls back to allow-all runtime policy","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:892-904 returns host_runtime_policy() when a zone has no configured policy, and crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1607-1633 feeds that permissive object straight into simulate_policy_decision() during live preflight. Attack scenario: an operator intends per-zone connector/capability deny rules but omits one zone from FCP_HOST_ZONE_POLICIES_FILE (or loses it during rollout); any caller holding an otherwise-valid capability token for that zone bypasses all rule-based connector/capability transport denials because the fallback policy has empty deny/ceiling lists and allow_lan/allow_derp/allow_funnel=true. Proposed mitigation: fail closed when a live request targets a zone with no configured policy, or require an explicit per-zone opt-in marker before using the permissive host_runtime_policy fallback.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:16:54.311369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:04.724768Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:04.724375Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hkhy","title":"[IDEA-17] Schema Hash + Canonical CBOR Vector Generator","description":"## Background
Spec and docs require deterministic CBOR + schema hash vectors, but maintaining them manually is error-prone. We need a generator to keep vectors and schema hashes in sync with the authoritative definitions.

## Goals
- Generate schema hashes and canonical CBOR vectors from source schemas.
- Emit stable JSON/CBOR fixtures for conformance tests.
- Integrate with CI to detect drift.

## Non-goals
- Auto-modify spec text or accept non-canonical encodings.

## Approach
- Add a generator tool in `fcp-conformance` (CLI subcommand).
- Parse schema definitions (CDDL/JSON schema or Rust type registry).
- Compute schema hash via existing canonicalization routines.
- Write vector files with deterministic ordering.

## Risks
- Schema evolution could break determinism; add fixtures and strict ordering rules.

## Success Criteria
- Generator produces identical outputs across runs/platforms.
- CI can regenerate and diff vectors without noise.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Regenerated vectors are byte‑for‑byte stable across two consecutive runs.
- Schema hash changes when a schema field definition changes.
- CI diff check fails on any vector drift.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Epic complete. Vector generator CLI works, determinism tests pass (16/16), CI vector regen+diff is implemented.","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hlk2h","title":"[FCP3/P1.1a] Inventory execution, lifecycle, and runtime ownership leakage","description":"Background: the original P1.1 bead was too large and too serial. Execution and runtime ownership leakage alone spans core, host, SDK, CLI, supervisor, and connector lifecycle surfaces, so collapsing it into one front door bead made the entire initiative less parallel than it needs to be.

Scope: inventory where invoke, stream, drain, checkpoint, resume, health, budgeting, supervision, and execution form neutrality are actually owned today. Call out which semantics live in fcp-core, which are re owned by fcp-host or fcp-sdk, which reach through into fwc, and which facts exist only as host local runtime state.

Acceptance: there is a self contained execution and lifecycle leakage inventory that later synthesis work can merge into the unified migration baseline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:37.168398Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:48.383129Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:48.382888Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","inventory","mesh-first","phase-1","runtime"],"comments":[{"id":2059,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hlk2h","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass 2026-03-27: Covered by docs/FCP3_Semantic_Ownership_Inventory.md sections 2-4. Execution leakage inventory: fcp-core owns 24 execution types + FcpConnector trait. fcp-host owns ProcessState machine, supervisor, resilience (CircuitBreaker/Bulkhead/LoadShedder), cancellation, progress. Key reach-throughs: rollout.rs↔LifecycleManager, enforcement.rs↔PolicyEngine. fwc reach-through: readiness.rs imports fcp-core introspection types.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:47Z"}]}
